title: Genomics and Ecology of Novel N<sub>2</sub>O-Reducing Microorganisms.
author_string: Hallin, Sara, Philippot, Laurent, Löffler, Frank E, Sanford, Robert A, Jones, Christopher M
abstract: Microorganisms with the capacity to reduce the greenhouse gas nitrous oxide (N<sub>2</sub>O) to harmless dinitrogen gas are receiving increased attention due to increasing N<sub>2</sub>O emissions (and our need to mitigate climate change) and to recent discoveries of novel N<sub>2</sub>O-reducing bacteria and archaea. The diversity of denitrifying and nondenitrifying microorganisms with capacity for N<sub>2</sub>O reduction was recently shown to be greater than previously expected. A formerly overlooked group (clade II) in the environment include a large fraction of nondenitrifying N<sub>2</sub>O reducers, which could be N<sub>2</sub>O sinks without major contribution to N<sub>2</sub>O formation. We review the recent advances about fundamental understanding of the genomics, physiology, and ecology of N<sub>2</sub>O reducers and the importance of these findings for curbing N<sub>2</sub>O emissions.
